task_completion.description: Wondershare EdrawMax is open and AWS-specific shapes are loaded in the library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The EdrawMax application window is visible. The left-hand library panel displays AWS icons (e.g., AWS Compute, AWS Network, AWS Storage).

task_completion.description: Page 1 depicts a Primary Region with a VPC, two Availability Zones, and a 3-tier architecture.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The canvas shows a large bounding box for a VPC, divided into at least two Availability Zones. Inside, there are distinct visual groupings or labels for Presentation, Application, and Data tiers.

task_completion.description: Page 1 includes all required networking and compute/storage components using AWS icons.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The diagram contains AWS icons representing an Internet Gateway, NAT Gateway, Route Tables, Load Balancers, Compute Instances (e.g., EC2), Managed Databases (e.g., RDS), and Object Storage (e.g., S3).

task_completion.description: A second page is created for Disaster Recovery or Multi-Region failover.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second page tab (e.g., 'Page 2' or 'DR') is visible at the bottom of the canvas area, and the active canvas displays a Disaster Recovery or secondary region architecture.

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application title bar shows 'aws_cloud_architecture.eddx' or a 'Save As' dialog box is visible showing the file being saved to '/home/ga/aws_cloud_architecture.eddx'.

integrity.description: The agent used the EdrawMax GUI to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the EdrawMax interface with menus, toolbars, and canvas being actively used, rather than a terminal window running a script to generate the file.

integrity.description: The diagram is constructed using individual shapes and connectors, not a pasted image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The canvas shows individual vector shapes, connection lines, and text boxes with selection handles or alignment guides visible in some screenshots, rather than a single flat image pasted from the internet.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply paste the prompt text into the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Text on the canvas consists of appropriate architectural labels (e.g., 'Presentation Tier', 'us-east-1a', 'Internet Gateway') rather than large blocks of text copied directly from the task description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output file must be saved exactly as '/home/ga/aws_cloud_architecture.eddx'. The document must contain at least 2 pages. Page 1 must depict a 3-tier architecture (Presentation, Application, Data) with VPC networking across 2 AZs. Page 2 must depict a Disaster Recovery or Multi-Region failover architecture. The diagram must use AWS-specific shapes.
